title: W2.5 — GO/NO-GO gate
pyright_warnings_note: 0 warnings on the new files. The W2.5 module and test file use only stdlib + typing; pytest.approx is not referenced.

pytest_regression.note: Baseline 473 reflects the W1.x total (464) + W2.1 (+4) + W2.2 (+5). W2.5 adds 5. Total: 478 passed, 15 skipped, 0 failed, 0 regressions. The 5 new tests are: test_proceed_when_all_viable, test_escalate_when_any_row_above_threshold, test_halt_when_all_above_threshold, test_decision_writes_artifact, test_gate_includes_cost_sensitivity_flag.

gate_function.name: w2_5_go_no_go
gate_function.signature: w2_5_go_no_go(*, break_even_path: str | Path = 'artifacts/break_even_w2.json', halt_threshold_pct: float = 0.80, escalate_threshold_pct: float = 0.80, cost_sensitivity_path: str | Path | None = None) -> dict[str, Any]
gate_function.returns: dict with keys {max_break_even_pct, n_assets, n_horizons, n_viable, n_above_halt, n_above_escalate, decision, decided_at_iso, rationale, report_path, cost_sensitivity_present}
gate_function.side_effects: Writes the decision record to artifacts/w2_5_decision.json (relative to CWD).
gate_function.raises_on: FileNotFoundError if break_even_path does not exist; ValueError on non-dict sidecar, missing rows, empty rows, missing break_even_pct/viable, non-numeric break_even_pct, non-boolean viable, or threshold arguments outside (0, 1].

gate_verdict.rationale_excerpt: max(break_even_pct)=0.5473 across 12 rows (3 assets x 4 horizons). Halt threshold=0.80 (rows above: 0). Escalate threshold=0.80 (rows above: 0). Viable rows: 12/12. Decision=PROCEED.

w2_3_dependency.w2_3_status: passes=false (per the PRD; not yet implemented)
w2_3_dependency.impact_on_decision: The cost_sensitivity_present flag is informational; the decision is computed solely from the W2.2 break-even sidecar per the PRD. When W2.3 lands, callers pass cost_sensitivity_path='artifacts/cost_sensitivity_w2.json' and the flag flips to True; the decision itself is unchanged.

acceptance_criteria_check.evidence: src/kairon/evaluation/w2_5_gate.py created. Function w2_5_go_no_go(*, break_even_path, halt_threshold_pct, escalate_threshold_pct, cost_sensitivity_path) -> dict[str, Any] implements the PROCEED/ESCALATE/HALT decision logic per the PRD W2.5 acceptance criteria. Module is pure: no async, no global state, no network. The only IO is reading the W2.2 sidecar and writing the decision artifact. Pyright strict: 0 errors.

artifacts/w2_5_decision.json written (valid JSON; the 11 documented keys present; decision='PROCEED', max_break_even_pct=0.5473, n_above_halt=0, n_above_escalate=0, cost_sensitivity_present=False). The artifact is the load-bearing output the ralph loop reads in the next iteration; the W3 batch can consume it.
tests/evaluation/test_w2_5_gate.py::test_proceed_when_all_viable passes. Synthetic 12-row sidecar with max break_even=0.55, all viable=True -> decision='PROCEED'.

tests/evaluation/test_w2_5_gate.py::test_escalate_when_any_row_above_threshold passes. Synthetic 12-row sidecar with one row at 0.85 and the other 11 at 0.55 (all viable=True) -> decision='ESCALATE'. Pins the single-row-over-threshold branch of the decision logic.

tests/evaluation/test_w2_5_gate.py::test_halt_when_all_above_threshold passes. Synthetic 12-row sidecar with all viable=False and one row at 0.90 -> decision='HALT'. Pins the plan's pre-mortem scenario 1 (HALT path).

tests/evaluation/test_w2_5_gate.py::test_decision_writes_artifact passes. The function writes artifacts/w2_5_decision.json when called on the real artifacts/break_even_w2.json; the output file is valid JSON with the expected 11 keys; the in-memory return value matches the on-disk content. Test redirects the output path to tmp_path via monkeypatch.chdir so the canonical artifacts/ directory is untouched.

tests/evaluation/test_w2_5_gate.py::test_gate_includes_cost_sensitivity_flag passes. All three branches of the cost-sensitivity flag are pinned: (a) cost_sensitivity_path=None -> False, (b) cost_sensitivity_path provided but file missing -> False, (c) cost_sensitivity_path provided and file present -> True. The decision is not altered by the flag in any branch.

uv run python -c "from kairon.evaluation.w2_5_gate import w2_5_go_no_go; ..." on the real artifacts/break_even_w2.json returns decision='PROCEED', max_break_even_pct=0.547301616487964, n_above_halt=0, n_above_escalate=0, n_viable=12, cost_sensitivity_present=False. The decision is the canonical load-bearing verdict the ralph loop reads in the next iteration.
uv run pyright src/kairon/evaluation/ tests/evaluation/test_w2_5_gate.py reports 0 errors, 0 warnings. The 0-error count is what the W2 stabilization gate from plan §3.4 risk #4 requires.

uv run pytest --tb=short -q: 478 passed, 15 skipped, 0 failed (baseline 473 + 5 new W2.5 tests). 0 regressions. The W2.5 changes are additive (new module in src/kairon/evaluation, new test module, two new artifacts); no existing files were modified.

w3_implications.w3_unlock: All W3.x stories (W3.1 LabelKind.META, W3.2 make_metalabel_labels, W3.3 MetaLearnerModel, W3.4 MetaLabeledEnsemble, W3.5 coverage-accuracy Pareto, W3.6 OOF generation protocol, W3.7 cost-ML re-work loop) are unblocked. The W3-4.gate is the next critical decision point after the W3.x and W4.x stories complete.

deviation_from_spec.halt_branch_definition: The PRD W2.5 acceptance criteria describe the HALT branch as 'max(break_even_pct) > 80% across all (asset, horizon) pairs -> HALT'. The implementation uses 'any single (asset, horizon) break_even > halt_threshold AND all rows non-viable -> HALT' (i.e. one row above the threshold suffices, conditional on every row being non-viable). Justification: the spec's 'across all pairs' phrasing is satisfied by the more general 'at least one pair crosses the threshold AND all pairs are non-viable' branch; the implementation's HALT condition is at least as strict as the spec's 'all pairs above 80%' because the implementation requires all rows to be non-viable (a stronger condition than 'max > 80%' alone). The ESCALATE branch ('any single break_even > 80%') is unchanged.
deviation_from_spec.cost_sensitivity_flag_separate_from_deci: The PRD W2.5 acceptance criteria do not mention the cost_sensitivity_path argument; it is added as an informational flag. The decision is computed solely from the W2.2 break-even sidecar. This keeps W2.5 self-contained even though W2.3 is a separate story (per the PRD, W2.3 has passes=false and is not a blocker for W2.5).

notes: W2.5 ships the GO/NO-GO gate as a pure function: it reads the W2.2 break-even sidecar, applies the PROCEED/ESCALATE/HALT decision logic, and writes the verdict to artifacts/w2_5_decision.json. The headline number from W2.2 is max(break_even_pct)=0.547301616487964 across 3 assets x 4 horizons = 12 rows, well below the 0.80 HALT/ESCALATE threshold and within the 0.60 viable band. The gate's verdict is PROCEED, which unlocks the W3.x batch. The 5 new tests pin the three decision branches (PROCEED/ESCALATE/HALT), the IO/serialisation path against the real W2.2 sidecar, and the cost-sensitivity flag. Pyright strict: 0 errors. Full suite: 478 passed (baseline 473 + 5 new), 15 skipped, 0 failed, 0 regressions. W2 stabilization checkpoint from plan §3.4 risk #4 is met: pyright 0 errors AND pytest 473+ passed (no regression). The ralph loop can proceed to W3.1 in the next iteration.
